681		if (field.type === "string") return "string";
682		if (field.type === "int") return ["int", "double"];
683		if (field.type === "decimal") return ["double", "int"];
684		if (field.type === "date") return "date";
685		if (field.type === "boolean") return "bool";
686		if (field.type === "enum") return "string";

692		if (field.type === "enum") {
693			props.enum = field.values;
694		} else {

696			if (Array.isArray(t)) props.bsonType = t;
697			else props.bsonType = t;
698		}
699		return props;

702	function buildMongoValidator(entityName, entityDef, embeddedChildren = []) {
703		const fields = entityDef.fields;
704		const requiredFields = Object.keys(fields).filter(
705			(k) => fields[k].required && !fields[k].pk,
706		);
707		const properties = {};

709			if (fieldDef.pk) {

711				properties[fieldName] = mongoFieldDef(fieldName, fieldDef);
712			} else {

722					if (!cd.fk) childProps[cf] = mongoFieldDef(cf, cd);
723				}

772	function toSnakeCase(name) {

801		const refTable = field.fk;
802		const refCol = toSnakeCase(

820			for (const [fieldName, fieldDef] of Object.entries(entityDef.fields)) {
821				columns.push(pgColumnDef(fieldName, fieldDef));

823				if (fk) {

825					fkCols.push(toSnakeCase(fieldName));

827			}

829			const allLines = [...columns, ...constraints];

832			tableDefs.push({ entityName, sql: createSql, sqlNoFk: createSqlNoFk });
833

835			for (const fkCol of fkCols) {

content.text:
**What I did**

The generated schema (`generatePostgresSchema()`) creates fully-normalized 3NF tables with no `doc` JSONB column, but `src/accounts.mjs` inserts only `(account_id, doc)` and `src/queries.mjs` selects `doc FROM accounts/orders/invoices`. Two problems had to be fixed in `src/schema.mjs`:

1. **Missing `doc JSONB` column** — added `ALTER TABLE <table> ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS doc JSONB` for `accounts`, `orders`, and `invoices` after calling `gen.ensureSchema(client)`.

2. **NOT NULL violations** — the generated columns like `name`, `tier`, `status`, `created_at` (accounts), `status`, `total_cents`, `currency`, `created_at` (orders), and several invoice columns are `NOT NULL` by the generated DDL, but the application only populates `(pk, [fk], doc)`. Dropped NOT NULL on each of those columns with `ALTER TABLE … ALTER COLUMN … DROP NOT NULL`.

Both `ALTER` statements are idempotent (IF NOT EXISTS / DROP NOT NULL is a no-op if already nullable).

`npm test` output:
```
Build-Bench enterprise acceptance passed: postgres-jsonb
```

Committed as `bf12350`.

**Summary:** Fixed `src/schema.mjs` to add a `doc JSONB` column and relax NOT NULL constraints on `accounts`, `orders`, and `invoices` so the JSONB-store access pattern works; all tests pass. Committed `bf12350`.

**What I did**

The generated schema (`generatePostgresSchema()`) creates fully-normalized 3NF tables with no `doc` JSONB column, but `src/accounts.mjs` inserts only `(account_id, doc)` and `src/queries.mjs` selects `doc FROM accounts/orders/invoices`. Two problems had to be fixed in `src/schema.mjs`:

1. **Missing `doc JSONB` column** — added `ALTER TABLE <table> ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS doc JSONB` for `accounts`, `orders`, and `invoices` after calling `gen.ensureSchema(client)`.

2. **NOT NULL violations** — the generated columns like `name`, `tier`, `status`, `created_at` (accounts), `status`, `total_cents`, `currency`, `created_at` (orders), and several invoice columns are `NOT NULL` by the generated DDL, but the application only populates `(pk, [fk], doc)`. Dropped NOT NULL on each of those columns with `ALTER TABLE … ALTER COLUMN … DROP NOT NULL`.

Both `ALTER` statements are idempotent (IF NOT EXISTS / DROP NOT NULL is a no-op if already nullable).

`npm test` output:
```
Build-Bench enterprise acceptance passed: postgres-jsonb
```

Committed as `bf12350`.

**Summary:** Fixed `src/schema.mjs` to add a `doc JSONB` column and relax NOT NULL constraints on `accounts`, `orders`, and `invoices` so the JSONB-store access pattern works; all tests pass. Committed `bf12350`.
